title: Parallel Sequencing of Barcoded BAC Clones by Random Tagging PCR
author: Elena Hilario
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p3adqie
materials: Make a master mix according to the following table, with at least 16 extra reactions: %s %s This is a dense solution. Aliquot 17 µL of master mix per well with a multichannel pipette into its corresponding PCR plate. No need to add BSA, it has no effect on the amplification and it only adds to the cost. Platinum Pfx has been discontinued (April 2018) and replaced by another enzyme blend called Superfi (Invitrogen cat# 12351010). If you prefer to use another enzyme mix, just make sure is a proofreading type.

steps.title: Transfer an aliquot of the BAC...
steps.instruction: Transfer an aliquot of the BAC library glycerol stock stored in the 384-well library plate using the 96-pin HDRT of the robotic unit into the 96-well conical bottom working plate which contains 150 μL of LB medium, 12.5 μg/mL tetracycline per well. Cover the plate with its corresponding lid and incubate at 37°C overnight. Thirty 384-well plates from the kiwifruit ( Actinidia chinensis ) BAC library were selected and represent 1X coverage of the genome (~750 Mbp). Each 384-well plate was divided in 4 quadrants to be transferred to 96-well working plates: A1 = quadrant 1; A2 = quadrant 2; B1 = quadrant 3; B2 = quadrant 4. A total of 120 quadrant plates were generated. Here we describe how to process one 384-well library plate (4 quadrants) only. You can scale it up to the desired number of library plates according to the level of coverage required. Use a liquid handling robotic unit to manipulate the BAC library plates. The robot should include a 96-pin high density replicating tool (HDRT). Undetermined, cell culture incubator

steps.title: The dataset reported in our paper...
steps.instruction: The dataset reported in our paper was obtained by preparing TruSeq Illumina libraries. Each plate pool was fragmented, end repaired, dA tailed and barcoded with Illumina adaptors. Ten plate libraries were pooled per lane and sequenced in the HiSeq2000 platform, 100 cycles, single end mode. See 'Guidelines and Warnings' section for comments on library and sequencing platform improvements developed since this method was created.

steps.title: Remove the BAC cell culture plates...
steps.instruction: Remove the BAC cell culture plates from the freezer and let the pellets defrost at room temperature for 10-20 min.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of resuspension buffer per well
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of resuspension buffer per well. Seal the plate with packaging tape and vortex for 10-20 seconds. Spin down briefly. The bacterial cell pellet should be completely dissolved.

steps.title: Add 200 μL of lysis solution...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of lysis solution per well and gently swirl the plate to ensure complete lysis. Incubate without shaking for 5 min at room temperature. 00:05:00

steps.title: Neutralize the lysate by adding 150...
steps.instruction: Neutralize the lysate by adding 150 μL of ice cold potassium acetate solution per well. Seal the plate with packaging tape and gently mix by vortexing at the lowest speed, 3-4 pulses of 5 s each. Keep the plates on ice for 10 min. 00:10:00 Undetermined, ice box

steps.title: Centrifuge the plates for 15 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the plates for 15 min at 3000 rpm, room temperature. 00:15:00, lysate centrifugation Most of the floculent material is collected at the bottom of the well. Don't worry if some is transferred to the fritted plate in the next step. go to step #3

steps.title: Transfer the lysate supernatant to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the lysate supernatant to a NUNC fritted plate assembled over a vacuum manifold containing its corresponding 2 mL 96-deep well receiving plate, in the same orientation. Cover the fritted plate with a silicone mat and apply vacuum to -10 in Hg for 2 min or until all liquid has been filtrated. Discard the fritted plate.

steps.title: Remove the receiving plate containing the...
steps.instruction: Remove the receiving plate containing the BAC plasmid and add 0.7 volumes of isopropanol per well at room temperature (approximately 260 μL) to each well. Seal the plate with packaging tape and vortex gently for 2-3 seconds, 3 times. Incubate at room temperature for 1 h. 01:00:00

steps.title: Collect the BAC plasmid DNA by...
steps.instruction: Collect the BAC plasmid DNA by centrifugation at 3000 rpm, 25 min at room temperature. 00:25:00, BAC plasmid DNA go to step #3

steps.title: Pour out the supernatant into a rectangular waste tray
steps.instruction: Pour out the supernatant into a rectangular waste tray. While the plate is still upside down, remove any traces of liquid by blotting it over paper towels for 2-3 seconds only. Dispose the isopropanol waste according to your local environmental regulations.

steps.title: Add 100 or 200 µL of...
steps.instruction: Add 100 or 200 µL of 70% ethanol to each well. Seal the plate with packaging tape and swirl it gently. Let it stand at room temperature for 10-20 min. Collect the BAC DNA by centrifugation at 3000 rpm, 25 min at room temperature. go to step #3

steps.title: Pour out the ethanol supernatant over...
steps.instruction: Pour out the ethanol supernatant over a rectangular tray and briefly blot the plate over paper towels. The ethanol waste can be poured down the drain and flushed with water for ~ 30 sec. Air dry the BAC pellet for ~ 10 min at room temperature. Add 150 µL of plasmid resuspension buffer per well, seal with packaging tape, spin down briefly and store at 4°C. The purpose of adding cresol red to the BAC plasmid DNA solution is to ensure the pH of the DNA solution is >7.5, and to visually confirm that the template transfer was successful in the following PCR reaction. The BAC plasmid solution should be red/pink, not yellow, which means it still contains sodium acetate and the pH is below 7.5. Weight the plate at t = 0 and after 5 and 10 min or until you see no change in weight. But if you prefer not to over dry the BAC DNA prep, add the buffer, seal the plate with packaging tape, vortex at medium speed and spin down briefly. Remove the tape and let any remaining ethanol evaporate at room temperature for 10-20 min. Cover the plate with a tissue paper (lint free) to prevent foreign objects fall into the wells.

steps.title: Transfer 20 μL of each well...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 μL of each well of the 96-well working plate to its corresponding address in a 2 mL 96-deep well plate filled with 1.2 mL LB medium and 12.5 µg/mL tetracycline per well. Seal the plate with breathable tape and incubate at 37°C in a cell culture shaker at 180 rpm for 16 h. 16:00:00 Undetermined, cell culture shaker incubator To increase the BAC plasmid yield, the plates can be stored in a cold room for ~ 6 h or overnight before harvesting the bacterial cells.

steps.title: Collect the bacterial cells by centrifugation...
steps.instruction: Collect the bacterial cells by centrifugation at 3000 rpm at room temperature for 25 min in a benchtop microtitre plate centrifuge. Remove the tape and pour out the medium by inverting the plate over a rectangular plastic box. Blot briefly over paper towels and seal with packaging tape. Store the plates containing the bacterial cells at -20°C until all the plates have been processed. To dispose the used medium transfer it to a flask and autoclave at 121°C for 15 min. After sterilization the medium can be poured down the drain. Load the plates according to their matching weights. Use rectangular pieces of paper the same size as the plates to match the weight values and place it under the plates.

steps.title: Make a master mix according to...
steps.instruction: Make a master mix according to the following table, with at least 16 extra reactions: %s %s Read note regarding pipetting dense solutions. Aliquot 44 µL of master mix per well into a PCR plate.

steps.title: Amplify as follows
steps.instruction: Amplify as follows: 94°C, 2 min → (94°C, 40 sec → 30°C, 2 min → 48°C, 4 min (set ramp at +0.1°C/sec) → 68°C, 1 min) x 50 cycles → 68°C, 7 min → stop and leave at room temperature (running time: 10 h) The annealing step is done at two temperatures: 30 and 48°C, with a very gentle ramp that will increase 0.1°C per second.

steps.title: For short term storage, keep the...
steps.instruction: For short term storage, keep the plate at 4°C, otherwise store it at -20°C. The PEP-PCR plates are kept at 4°C until all the BAC plasmid preps have been processed. One microlitre of 2% xylene cyanole FF solution can be added to each PEP-PCR reaction before the next round of amplification to visually confirm the addition of template.

steps.title: Amplify as follows
steps.instruction: Amplify as follows: 94°C, 2 min → (94°C, 30 sec → Touchdown 68°C-58°C, 30 sec at -0.5°C/cycle → 68°C, 1 min) x 20 cycles → (94°C, 30 sec → 58°C, 30 sec → 68°C, 1 min) x 10 cycles → 68°C, 7 min → stop and leave at room temperature.

steps.title: Store the PCR plates at 4°C...
steps.instruction: Store the PCR plates at 4°C until all plates have been processed and you are ready to prepare the plate pools.

steps.title: Add 1 μL of its corresponding...
steps.instruction: Add 1 μL of its corresponding t-BACRB oligonucleotide to each well. Add 2 μL of PEP-PCR solution to its corresponding well. Seal the plate with aluminium tape and vortex vigorously. Spin down briefly.

steps.title: Add 1 μL of its corresponding...
steps.instruction: Add 1 μL of its corresponding BACRB oligonucleotide to each well. Add 5 μL of BAC plasmid solution to its corresponding well. Seal the plate with packaging tape and vortex vigorously. Spin down briefly. Add 20 µL of sterile mineral oil to each well with a multichannel pipette, seal the plate with aluminium tape. The addition of mineral oil should prevent evaporation but check all wells, especially the ones at the edge of the plate. If less than half of the total volume of the reaction has evaporated, add enough 1X TE pH 7.5 buffer to bring back the volume to 50 µL. Repeat the PEP-PCR reaction if more than half of the liquid has evaporated. Avoid touching the rim of the well with mineral oil because it will prevent the aluminium tape stick properly.

steps.title: Transfer the contents of each 96-well...
steps.instruction: Transfer the contents of each 96-well plate (one quadrant) into a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube (final volume ~ 1.8 mL) and analyze 20 μL by agarose gel electrophoresis (2% agarose in 1X TAE buffer, 1 kb+ ladder, 140 V, constant current, 30 min. Stain with SybrSafe 10 min (3 μL SybrSafe in 50 mL 1X TAE).

steps.title: Transfer 700 μL of each quadrant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 700 μL of each quadrant pool into a 15 mL conical tube (final volume 2.8 mL). Add 280 μL of 3 M sodium acetate pH 5.2 to each tube and vortex briefly. Add 1.96 mL isopropanol per tube at room temperature and vortex until completely mixed. Incubate at room temperature for 1 h. 01:00:00, isopropanol precipitation go to step #12

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 3000 rpm for 25 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 3000 rpm for 25 min. Discard the isopropanol supernatant and add 1 mL 70% ethanol per tube. Make sure the pellet is detached from the tube and keep at room temperature for ~ 10 min. Centrifuge again at the same speed and time. go to step #3

steps.title: Remove the ethanol supernatant and blot...
steps.instruction: Remove the ethanol supernatant and blot the tube on paper towels without losing the pellet. Air dry the pellet ~ 10 min and add 300 µL TE buffer per tube. go to step #14

steps.title: Run 2 μL of each plate...
steps.instruction: Run 2 μL of each plate pool in a 2% agarose gel in 1X TAE buffer. go to step #24

steps.title: Quantify at least 12 BAC plasmid...
steps.instruction: Quantify at least 12 BAC plasmid preps from each quadrant plate (48 BAC preps per library plate). Use 1 μL of BAC plasmid for the Qubit HS dsDNA kit. Take the average value of those 48 reactions and if needed adjust the concentration to ~ 3 ng/uL by adding more plasmid resuspension buffer.
